commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=fbf64d3c20455b16ef75d5719397b5708ba77248)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a `smoke-ci` QA profile run and artifact upload to the existing `checks-fast-bundled-protocol` CI shard, with a workflow guard test for that wiring.

PR surface: Tests +34, Config +25. Total +59 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the PR defect by source and PR-proof inspection: the added required shard exits with the `smoke-ci` exit code, and the PR body reports the full `smoke-ci` run failing 27 scenarios. I did not run local tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] A maintainer should decide whether to gate a green subset, wait for full-profile readiness, or intentionally accept the required-CI failure; that policy choice is not a safe automated repair.

target.title: ci: run QA smoke profile in existing CI shard
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

PR #95880 generalized QA profile evidence generation, but this smoke proof should run through normal CI planning rather than a separate manual wrapper or a category fanout. This PR folds the mock-backed `smoke-ci` QA profile into the existing `checks-fast-bundled-protocol` CI shard.

## Why This Change Was Made

`smoke-ci` uses mock-provider mode, so it does not need `OPENAI_API_KEY`. The CI workflow now builds the private QA runtime and runs `pnpm openclaw qa run --qa-profile smoke-ci` from the existing `bundled-protocol` fast-core task, then uploads `.artifacts/qa-e2e/smoke-ci-profile/` from that same job.

The workflow does not re-list smoke category IDs from `taxonomy.yaml`, does not add `qa_category` matrix entries, and does not create `checks-fast-qa-smoke-profile-*` checks. The guard test asserts those constraints so category taxonomy stays single-sourced in `taxonomy.yaml`.

## User Impact

No product runtime behavior changes. Full Node CI now produces QA smoke evidence from an existing fast-core shard without registering one runner per QA category. Scenario failures preserve the QA exit code while the `always()` artifact upload still keeps evidence for debugging.

## Evidence

Current PR head: `fbf64d3c20`.

Local checks on the updated branch:

- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/ci-workflow-guards.test.ts`
- `go run github.com/rhysd/actionlint/cmd/actionlint@v1.7.11 .github/workflows/ci.yml .github/workflows/qa-profile-evidence.yml .github/workflows/maturity-scorecard.yml`
- `node scripts/check-no-conflict-markers.mjs`
- `python3 scripts/check-composite-action-input-interpolation.py`
- `uvx zizmor==1.22.0 --config .github/zizmor.yml --persona=regular --min-severity=medium --min-confidence=medium .github/workflows/ci.yml .github/workflows/qa-profile-evidence.yml .github/workflows/maturity-scorecard.yml`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main --parallel-tests "git diff --check origin/main...HEAD && node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/ci-workflow-guards.test.ts && go run github.com/rhysd/actionlint/cmd/actionlint@v1.7.11 .github/workflows/ci.yml .github/workflows/qa-profile-evidence.yml .github/workflows/maturity-scorecard.yml"` returned clean with no accepted/actionable findings.

Local smoke timings/proof:

- Full unsplit `smoke-ci`: 1631s, selected 34 categories / 86 scenarios, produced evidence but failed 59 passed / 27 failed.
- Surface split trial for `agent-runtime-and-provider-execution`: stopped after about 8 minutes; it selected 6 categories / 55 scenarios.
- Category split trial for `agent-runtime-and-provider-execution.agent-turn-execution`: selected 1 category / 15 scenarios and eventually hit a local gateway startup health failure after 4023s on a heavily loaded workstation.
- Narrow category proof `gateway-runtime.websocket-connection`, no `--allow-failures`, including `qaRuntime` build: 14s and passed. This proves native `execution.kind=vitest` QA scenarios work through this profile path.

I cancelled the superseded run that used the rejected category fanout (`28080234325`) after it created one check per QA category.

## Linked Context

Related #94276
Related #94283
Related #94306
Related #95880
Related #95971

## Risk Checklist

- User-visible behavior: no product runtime behavior changed.
- Config/environment/migration behavior: no runtime config or migration behavior changed.
- Security/auth/secrets/tool execution: CI smoke does not pass live provider secrets and relies on mock-provider mode.
- Highest-risk area: adding full QA smoke work to an existing Node CI shard.
- Mitigation: no new runner fanout, artifact upload scoped to `bundled-protocol`, required-check failure on scenario failure, and a guard test tying the workflow to the `smoke-ci` taxonomy profile without duplicating category IDs.
target.author: solvely-colin

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge

commands.author: Solvely-Colin
